rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH VS. NORTENO A CRIMINAL STREET GANG SUED AS AN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Norteno (California Civil Code Section 3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 533)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH Notice Of Motion To Modify Permanent Injunction Related To Norteno (California Civil Code Section 3424, California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 533). People of the State of California's motion to modify permanent injunction related to Norteno is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUDEN VS. C.R. BARD, INC., A NEW JERSEY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF WAYNE RUDEN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Continued to July 10, 2018 to give plaintiff Wayne Ruden an opportunity to file a reply memorandum in response to the opposition memorandum filed by defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals. For reasons that are not clear, Mr. Ruden's counsel mistakenly believes that CPMC did not oppose this motion. Mr. Ruden's reply memorandum must be filed no later than July 5, 2018 and a courtesy copy provided to department 302 the same day. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants/Cross-Complainants, Martin Mcnerney Development, Inc. Dba Martin Building Company'S, Fifth Historic Properties, Llc'S, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, Llc'S, And 418 Jessie Properties, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Limited Motion To Contest Broadway Mechanical-Contractors, Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Order Dismissing And Barring Cross-Complaints; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Sergio W. Stevens In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC, Motion And Limited Motion To Contest Broadway Mechanical-Contractors, Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Order Dismissing And Barring Cross-Complaints. Defendants and cross-complainants Martin McNerney Development, Inc., Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC's motion to contest good faith settlement is denied. The movants acknowledge that the settlement is in good faith and bars further claims for equitable contribution or equitable indemnity. While a good faith determination bars claims of implied contractual indemnity, it does not bar the movants from continuing to prosecute their first, fourth and fifth causes of action in their cross-complaint. Those claims can be construed as claims for express contractual indemnity. The court cannot resolve on this motion the contractual interpretation issues that the parties' pleadings raise. A motion for summary judgment/adjudication or a trial is necessary to resolve the scope of the parties' respective express indemnity rights and obligations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Broadway is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN MASSEY VS. NEVIN EBERHARDT-SANDSTROM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Cal Wellness'S Motion For Dismissal, Or In The Alternative To Quash.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 12, Specially Appearing Defendant Cal Wellness'S Motion For Dismissal, Or In The Alternative To Quash. Specially appearing defendant Cal Wellness's motion for dismissal is denied and its alternative motion to quash service of the second amended complaint on counsel for Cal Wellness is granted. The apparent mistake of counsel for plaintiff Stephen Massey in believing that service of the second amended complaint on counsel for Cal Wellness was sufficient to require Cal Wellness to respond to the second amended complaint is not a basis to preclude Mr. Massey from seeking any relief against Cal Wellness, especially given the lack of any legal prejudice to Cal Wellness and California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. If, as it appears, counsel for Cal Wellness is not authorized or willing to accept service of the summons and second amended complaint on behalf of Cal Wellness, then Mr. Massey will need to effect valid service on Cal Wellness. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Massey is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To The Cross-Complaint Against No More Dirt, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT FAIRMONT HOTEL MANAGEMENT, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To The Cross-Complaint Against No More Dirt, Inc.. Cross-complainant Fairmont Hotel Management, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication against cross-defendant No More Dirt, Inc. is continued to July 12, 2018 to give No More Dirt an opportunity to file a sur-reply memorandum in response to the evidence set forth in FHML's reply papers on the issue of standing. While FHML did not submit any evidence in its moving papers that it has standing to file a claim against No More Dirt under the indemnity provision as Fairmont Hotel Company - San Francisco, L.P.'s exclusive agent, the court has discretion to consider new evidence in reply papers on a motion for summary judgment, provided that the other party has notice and opportunity to respond. (Weiss v. Chevron, U.S.A, Inc. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1094, 1098; Plenger v. Alza Corp. (1992) 11 CalApp.4th 349, 362 n 8.) No More Dirt must file its sur-reply memorandum no later July 6, 2018 limited to the evidence FHML submitted in the reply papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP VS. LAMA ASHKAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Supplemental responses to be served by July 17, 2018. Sanctions denied as there was an apparent miscommunication between counsel as to whether this motion would be deferred during Defendant's counsel's vacation. Further meet and confer discussions could have obviated this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (HK/302/JPT).

rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES ET AL VS. SEAN KILLEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Order Imposing Evidentiary Sanctions (Defendant Quattrin)
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANTS SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, ANDREW QUATTRIN, NEW BERNAL HEIGHTS, INC.'S Motion For Relief From Order Imposing Evidentiary Sanctions (Defendant Quattrin) Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for relief granted under CCP 473(b). The Court finds that inadvertence, mistake, surprise or neglect of counsel has been shown through the various declarations. Plaintiff's requests for disclosure of Mr. Benjamin's retainer and imposition of additional sanctions are not at issue herein, but may be raised without prejudice in another motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (HK/302/JPT).

rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES ET AL VS. SEAN KILLEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration (Defendant Quattrin)
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANTS SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, ANDREW QUATTRIN, NEW BERNAL HEIGHTS, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration (Defendant Quattrin) Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for reconsideration granted under CCP 1008. The Court's prior order explicitly states that evidentiary sanctions were required because no documents had been produced and that the April trial date was quickly approaching. Given the continuance of the trial date and the apparent production of documents, Plaintiffs will no longer be prejudiced by Defendants' conduct. In addition, defendants' opposition contains new facts relating to the circumstances why no appearance was made at the hearing on the motion. The combination of these new facts are sufficient for relief under CCP 1008. The original motion for terminating and evidentiary sanctions is now denied as moot given the payment of sanctions and the production of documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (HK/302/JPT).

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for reconsideration granted under CCP 1008. The Court's prior order explicitly states that evidentiary sanctions were required because no documents had been produced and that the April trial date was quickly approaching. Given the continuance of the trial date and the apparent production of documents, Plaintiffs will no longer be prejudiced by Defendants' conduct. In addition, defendants' opposition contains new facts relating to the circumstances why no appearance was made at the hearing on the motion. The combination of these new facts are sufficient for relief under CCP 1008. The original motion for terminating and evidentiary sanctions is now denied as moot given the payment of sanctions and the production of documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (HK/302/JPT).

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. BRYANT FU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Crystal Lei And Bryant Fu'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexaious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 29, 2018, Line 13, Defendants Crystal Lei And Bryant Fu'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexaious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security. Hearing required. The court re-issues the tentative ruling that was issued prior to the June 26 hearing. Defendants Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu's motion for an order requiring vexatious litigant Demas Yan to post security is granted. Per CCP 391.3 and 391.4, unless Mr. Yan furnishes acceptable security in the amount of $50,000 for the benefit of Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu no later than July 31, 2018, this case will be dismissed as to Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu on August 1, 2018. Per CCP 391.6, this case is stayed until July 31, 2018 or the date as Mr. Yan furnishes the required security, whichever occurs first. Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu have shown that Mr. Yan is a vexatious litigant within the meaning of CCP 391(b) and that there is no reasonable possibility that Mr. Yan will prevail in this case. $50,000 is the minimum reasonable amount that Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu are likely to incur to defend themselves in this case. Although Mr. Yan is represented by an attorney in this case, that representation does not preclude Mr. Yan from the posting security requirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
